bio.extra_names: Birthdate discrepancy: Wikipedia article body says Aug 8, 1833; infobox says Aug 3, 1833 — going with Aug 8 (article body).

death.note: Died 8 days after his wife Addie Miller Ferry — close-coupled spousal deaths.

burial.plot_notes: Woodmere Cemetery, Detroit Wayne Co MI

parents.father_research_notes: Joseph N. Ferry — wagonmaker, died when Dexter was 3.
parents.mother_research_notes: Lucy Mason Ferry — moved family to Penfield NY after husband's death.

careers.notes: Per Wikipedia: started 1852 at age 19 after time as farm laborer + employment with Ezra M. Parsons of Rochester NY 1851. Promoted to salesman, then bookkeeper.

careers.employer: M.T. Gardner & Company / Gardner, Ferry, and Church
careers.notes: Joined the seed-growing business as junior partner with Milo T. Gardner + Eber F. Church. First year: $6,000 in business. Built reputation.

Bought out Gardner's share 1865; renamed Ferry, Church & Co; renamed D.M. Ferry & Co. 1867 after Church's retirement. Innovations: only fresh seed (better germination), seeds in small packets. Incorporated 1879 with $750,000 capital. By 1890s doing $1.5M annually; by early 1900s $2M+ annually supplying 160,000 retail outlets. Survived a near-total $1M warehouse fire Jan 1, 1886. AT ONE TIME THE LARGEST SEED COMPANY IN THE WORLD. Predecessor of Ferry-Morse Seed Co.

careers.employer: First National Bank of Detroit + Union Trust Company + Standard Accident Insurance + Michigan Fire & Marine Insurance + American Harrow Co + National Pin Co
careers.notes: Multiple presidencies during peak business career.

careers.employer: Olivet College + First Congregational Church of Detroit + Harper Hospital (40 years) + Grace Hospital (founder)
Detroit Museum of Art (predecessor to Detroit Institute of Arts)
careers.notes: DIA founding subscriber 1884 — establishing the Ferry family's century-long association with the Detroit Institute of Arts. Daughter Blanche's brother Dexter Jr. presided over DIA 1914-1917.
Per Wikipedia: 'on the Detroit Board of Park Commissioners in 1884.'

One term Detroit Board of Estimates 1877-78; delegate to Republican National Convention 1892 + 1904; Chair Republican State Central Committee 1896-1898; candidate for Republican nomination for Michigan Governor 1900.

sources.short: Findagrave: Dexter Mason Ferry (Woodmere Cemetery Detroit)

narrative_blocks.header: Built world's largest seed company in Detroit
narrative_blocks.body: He went from errand boy in 1852 to renaming the firm D.M. Ferry & Co. in 1867 — by 1900 the largest seed company in the world, doing over $2M annually through 160,000 retail outlets. He pioneered fresh-seed quality control and small consumer packets.

narrative_blocks.header: Founding subscriber to the Detroit Institute of Arts
narrative_blocks.body: He was a founding subscriber to the Detroit Museum of Art in 1884 — predecessor to the Detroit Institute of Arts. His son Dexter Jr. served as its president 1914-1917, and his granddaughter Blanchette Hooker Rockefeller would later become MoMA president.

narrative_blocks.header: His daughter Blanche married Elon Huntington Hooker
narrative_blocks.body: His daughter Blanche Ferry married Elon Huntington Hooker, the Niagara Electrochemical Company founder. The seed-empire fortune flowed through her into the Hooker line and eventually to her granddaughter Blanchette Ferry Hooker Rockefeller.

narrative_blocks.header: Died eight days after his wife
narrative_blocks.body: Addie Miller Ferry died first in late 1906; Dexter followed just eight days later. They had been married thirty-nine years.

narrative_blocks.header: $1M warehouse fire on New Year's Day 1886
narrative_blocks.body: On January 1, 1886, his Detroit warehouse burned to the ground in a near-total $1M loss. He absorbed two competitors and filled all outstanding orders without interruption.
